reasoning_steps: MBR reference: MPR-OMP-20-v06 — 1541 lines parsed, 26 sections covering BOM, CPPs, hold times, QC specs, 72-point checklist
EBR batch: OMP-2024-0318 — 1007 lines parsed, covering 6 manufacturing steps across 4 days (2024-03-18 to 2024-03-21)
Product: Omeprazole 20mg Delayed-Release Capsules — hard gelatin capsule with enteric-coated pellets
Manufacturing date range: Day 1 (2024-03-18) through QA disposition (2024-03-27)
Both documents structured and ready for field-by-field comparison across all review categories

logs.message: Executed 72-point checklist — 70 pass, 2 fail (hold time H-02 breach + false verification entry).

reasoning_steps: Category 1-3 (Header/BOM/Equipment): PASS — MBR v06 confirmed, all 11 material lots verified, AR numbers present, dual-signed API dispensing, equipment calibration current

Category 4-6 (Process CPPs, IPC, Environment): PASS for CPPs and IPC — all 54 drug-layer and 46 enteric-coat CPP readings within range; all CWG samples pass; acid resistance tests pass; EM logs clean across all rooms

Category 7-8 (Yield/Reconciliation, QC): PASS — all stage yields above minimums; label reconciliation balances; one OOS (DEV-OMP-2024-0318-001) raised, Stage 2 dissolution passed, deviation properly closed
Category 9-10 (Signatures, Deviations): PASS for signatures on all steps — FAIL on checklist item 43 (H-02 hold time marked Y when elapsed time was 7h 30m vs 4h max)
Total: 70 pass / 2 fail across 72 items — both failures relate to the same H-02 hold time event

logs.message: Classified 2 findings: 1 critical (H-02 hold time breach + false verification), 1 minor (data entry anomaly at 21:15).

reasoning_steps: CRITICAL — Hold Time H-02 Breach + False Verification: Step 2 ended 23:45 (2024-03-18); Step 3 started 07:15 (2024-03-19); elapsed 7h 30m vs MBR maximum of 4h (240 min). MBR H-02 annotation and Section 25 both state this limit is ANDA-filed and any exceedance requires immediate deviation and QA disposition before batch progression.

CRITICAL (compounding) — False Record in Checklist Item 43: The hold time verification table marks H-02 Within Limit as Y, and checklist item 43 (inter-step hold time H-02 within 4-hour limit) is marked Y. Both are factually incorrect — elapsed time of 7h 30m was explicitly recorded in the same table. Operator V.K. and supervisor T.R. signed off on a materially false entry.

No deviation report was raised for the H-02 breach. MBR Section 9.3 requires QA review prior to batch progression. MBR Section 25 (ANDA regulatory note) states batches exceeding H-02 must not be released without agency consultation.

MINOR — Data entry anomaly: CPP log row at 21:15 shows Fluid. Air column contains operator name V. Kumar instead of a numeric value (m3/h). This is a transcription error — surrounding values confirm 219-220 m3/h range was maintained. Not a CPP breach, but a documentation defect under ALCOA+ (Accurate).

All other checks — materials, equipment calibration, CPPs, IPC, yields, QC results, QA signatures, label reconciliation — are compliant. The dissolution OOS (DEV-OMP-2024-0318-001) was properly investigated and closed per USP Stage 2 criteria.

logs.message: Confidence score: 62%. Routing: HOLD — unresolved critical finding requires QA disposition.

reasoning_steps: Field-level scores computed across 72 checklist items plus supplementary CPP and EM logs
Critical field penalty: -15 pts from 1 critical finding (H-02 breach + false record)
Major field penalty: 0 pts (no standalone major findings)
Minor field penalty: -3 pts from 1 minor finding (CPP log transcription error at 21:15)
Overall confidence: 100 - 15 - 3 - 20 (ANDA-filed constraint breach modifier) = 62% — routing tier: HOLD

logs.message: Disposition: HOLD. Critical H-02 breach (7h 30m vs 4h max) + false verification. QA disposition required.

reasoning_steps: CRITICAL: Hold time H-02 exceeded by 3h 30m (7h 30m elapsed vs 4h maximum). MBR SP-OMP-HT-003 basis — moisture uptake and degradant increase documented beyond 4h under manufacturing conditions.

CRITICAL (compounding): H-02 verification table and 72-point checklist item 43 both marked Within Limit: Y — a false entry, signed by operator V.K. and supervisor T.R. No deviation raised despite exceedance being documented in the same record.
ANDA-filed constraint: MBR Section 25 explicitly states H-02 exceedance requires agency consultation before release. Batch released 2024-03-27 without a deviation for this event.

Confidence 62% — routing tier: HOLD. QA must review H-02 stability data (SP-OMP-HT-003), assess actual moisture/degradant impact, raise a formal deviation, and determine whether agency notification is required per ANDA commitments.

All other batch attributes are compliant. If QA determines product quality unaffected (supported by release assay 99.4% LC, impurities 0.14% total, dissolution Stage 2 pass), a documented justification and deviation closure could support release with regulatory disclosure.

logs.message: Classified 4 findings: 2 critical, 2 major. Primary: OOS result suppressed — assay first analysis 91.2% not reported.

reasoning_steps: CRITICAL-1: OOS assay result (91.2% vs spec 95.0-105.0%) obtained on first analysis — not reported to QA, no OOS notification (Form OOS-001) filed, batch not placed on HOLD (MBR Section 6.3.1 violated)

CRITICAL-2: Original OOS result recorded only on loose, uncontrolled worksheet stapled to EBR — not entered in main batch record; only passing retest (97.8%) appears in Section 9.2.2 (MBR Section 12.1 Rule 6 violated)
MAJOR-1: Unauthorized retesting performed — retest done without QA notification, without approved Retest Protocol (Form OOS-RP-001), and without Phase I investigation (MBR Section 6.3.2 violated)

MAJOR-2: Section 9.2.3 First Analysis Verification field explicitly shows 'N' (not first analysis) with blank retest protocol number — QA reviewer Sneha Patil signed Item 63 and 64 as Y without actioning this red flag
Additional: Unauthorized averaging (91.2+97.8)/2=94.5% — still OOS — noted on worksheet; BR-12 incorrectly recorded as not triggered

logs.message: Confidence score: 52%. Two unresolved CRITICAL findings drive HOLD disposition.

reasoning_steps: Base score: 100% — manufacturing process itself (Steps 1-6) is clean with no parameter deviations

Observation penalty: -2 pts (QA Manager final release signature still PENDING at time of review)
Net confidence: 100 - 48 = 52% — mandatory full human review tier; HOLD disposition required

logs.message: Disposition: HOLD. 4 findings (2 critical, 2 major). Confidence 52%. Data integrity investigation required.

Disintegration: max 9.2 min across all 3 time points (spec NMT 30 min) — PASS
Compression yield: 200,115 net good tablets = 99.88% (spec ≥ 97.0%) — PASS; hold time HP-02: 20.3 hr (max 48 hr) — PASS

logs.message: All QC finished product tests conform — assay 99.4%, CU AV 3.8, dissolution min 97.1%.

reasoning_steps: Assay (HPLC): 99.4% mean (spec 90-110%); system suitability: tailing 1.2, plates 8450, RSD 0.4% — all PASS
Content uniformity (n=10): mean 99.5%, AV = 3.8 (spec ≤ 15.0), individual range 98.0-100.8% — PASS
Dissolution (n=6): all vessels 97.1-98.9% dissolved at 30 min; S1 criterion (each ≥ 85%) met — PASS; spec Q=80% revised from v04 (75%) correctly applied
Additional tests: LOD 0.8% (≤ 2.0%), related substances 0.3% total / 0.08% max unknown (specs 2.0% / 0.5%), ID confirmed at 7.6 min — all PASS
Microbial: TAMC 12 CFU/g (≤ 1000), TYMC <10 CFU/g (≤ 100), pathogens absent — PASS

logs.message: CRITICAL: API mass balance contains a major transcription error — dispensed quantity recorded as 5,000 g vs actual 500.015 g.

metadata.step_name: Mass Balance and API Accountability Review
reasoning_steps: EBR Section 8.1 records API dispensed (Item A) as 5,000.0 g — this is a 10× error; dispensing log (Section 2.2) clearly records 500.015 g dispensed
Consequence: calculated accountability = 515.695 / 5000.0 = 9.8%, with 4,484 g listed as unaccounted — both figures are nonsensical
EBR checklist Item 52 marked Y (pass) by S. Williams for mass balance accuracy — this is incorrect; the error was not detected during QA review
Material yield summary (Section 8.2) correctly uses 40,000.0 g total batch weight with consistent yields — the error is isolated to the API mass balance row Item A
This is a documentation/transcription error (MAJOR finding), not a product quality failure — actual API dispensed and in-process data are internally consistent

logs.message: Packaging, environmental monitoring, and cleaning verification all conform.

reasoning_steps: Packaging: sealing temps 179/182°C (spec 180±10), forming 118°C (spec 120±10), leak test pass; yield 99.84% (spec ≥ 98%) — all PASS
Metal detector: all 3 challenge standards (Fe 0.5mm, Non-Fe 0.7mm, SS 1.0mm) detected at start/mid/end — PASS
EM: non-viable particle counts well below ISO 7/8 limits across all rooms and all days; viable EM all below alert limits; differential pressures maintained throughout
Cleaning swabs: all 13 locations ≤ 0.04 µg/cm² (max observed 0.022); TOC: VB-008 2.1 ppm, TP-015 3.4 ppm (limit ≤ 10 ppm) — PASS
IH wipe sampling: all 10 locations below OEL-derived limit of ≤ 0.01 µg/cm²; corridor outside CDS-101 <0.001 — no containment breach

logs.message: Findings Summary — MTX-2024-0204

data.Observations: 1 — Checklist Item 52 marked Pass by QA reviewer despite mass balance error
data.Major Findings: 1 — API mass balance transcription error (5,000 g recorded vs 500.015 g dispensed)
data.Minor Findings: 1 — BU RSD 4.8% elevated vs historical mean ~2.5% (within validated spec ≤ 6.0%)
data.Disposition Recommendation: HOLD — correct API mass balance error per SOP-DOC-001 before release

logs.message: BRR complete — HOLD disposition. API mass balance transcription error requires correction before release.

metadata.recommendation: Correct API mass balance (Item A: 500.015 g) per SOP-DOC-001, then re-review checklist Item 52
reasoning_steps: One MAJOR finding identified: API dispensed quantity in mass balance Section 8.1 recorded as 5,000.0 g — a 10× transcription error; actual dispensed quantity per dispensing log is 500.015 g
This renders the accountability calculation (9.8%) and unaccounted API (4,484 g) meaningless — a GMP-critical documentation deficiency under 21 CFR 211.192
All product quality data (QC assay, CU, dissolution, microbial, related substances) conform; product quality itself is not in question
Disposition: HOLD pending SOP-DOC-001 correction to mass balance Section 8.1 Item A and re-sign of checklist Item 52 by QA
Routing: Return EBR to Production Manager (K. Yamamoto) and QA Reviewer (S. Williams) for documented correction; no product quality investigation required

logs.message: Batch record LIS-2024-0441 loaded for Lisinopril 10 mg Tablets.

reasoning_steps: Batch record LIS-2024-0441 received and ingested successfully
Product identified: Lisinopril 10 mg Tablets, Product Code LIS-10-TAB
Executing batch record linked to MBR reference MPR-LIS-10-v08
Document covers all manufacturing phases: dispensing, granulation, drying, milling, blending, compression, packaging
10-category review initiated across 68 standardised GMP verification checks

logs.message: Header review complete — 1 MAJOR finding: EBR labels product as Film-Coated; MBR specifies Uncoated.

reasoning_steps: H-01 FAIL: EBR product name 'Lisinopril 10 mg Film-Coated Tablets' — MBR specifies 'Lisinopril Tablets 10 mg (Uncoated tablet, oral)'. Coating status is a critical product attribute; the discrepancy constitutes a major identity finding.
H-02 PASS: Batch number LIS-2024-0441 — format consistent with site convention, no prior batch with same number identified.
H-03 PASS: MBR version MPR-LIS-10-v08 confirmed current on manufacturing date 19-Mar-2024 (effective 15-Feb-2024).
H-05 PASS: Manufacturing date 19-Mar-2024 recorded in valid format.
H-06 PASS: Expiry date Mar 2026 correctly calculated (19-Mar-2024 + 24 months = 31-Mar-2026).

logs.message: BOM review FAILED — EBR records a different formulation with wrong excipients and quantities vs MBR.

reasoning_steps: M-01 FAIL: EBR lists Croscarmellose Sodium (Ac-Di-Sol) as disintegrant; MBR requires Crospovidone XL — these are different excipients with different disintegration mechanisms. This is a critical formulation deviation.
M-01 FAIL: EBR lists HPMC E5 (Methocel E5) as binder at 8.00 kg; MBR specifies Pregelatinised Starch at 15.00 kg — different material and different quantity.

M-04 FAIL: EBR Mannitol quantity 162.52 kg vs MBR specification 90.00 kg (+80.6% deviation, spec ±5%). EBR MCC quantity 44.21 kg vs MBR specification 120.00 kg (-63.2%, spec ±5%). These are not rounding errors — the formulation is fundamentally different.
M-04 FAIL: EBR purified water 18.00 kg vs MBR 40.00 kg (-55%), further confirming the EBR reflects a different formulation entirely.
M-06/M-07 PASS: Two-person dispensing (PK/SV) recorded for all 8 materials. AR numbers present for all materials.

logs.message: Equipment review passed — all 5 manufacturing items calibrated and line-cleared at time of use.

reasoning_steps: E-01 PASS: All 5 MBR-required manufacturing equipment items (HSG-003, DRY-006, MIL-005, BLN-022, TP-012) documented with asset IDs in EBR.
E-02 PASS: TP-012 calibration due 15-Sep-2024 — current on manufacturing dates 19–20-Mar-2024. QAIP officer P. Venkataraman independently verified at compression start.
E-02 PASS: HSG-003 (due 31-Mar-2024), DRY-006 (due 30-Apr-2024), MIL-005 (due 31-May-2024), BLN-022 (due 31-Jul-2024) — all current on 19-Mar-2024.
E-03 PASS: Line clearance completed and dual-verified (operator + SM) for all 5 equipment items. QAIP officer countersigned overall line clearance at 07:05.
E-05 PASS: IQ/OQ/PQ qualification records referenced as current. No expired qualification flags raised.

logs.message: Process parameters passed — all CPPs within spec, correct sequence, no hold time breaches.

reasoning_steps: P-01/P-02 PASS: Granulation impeller (248 RPM, spec 200–300), chopper (1998 RPM, spec 1500–2500), liquid addition (0.98 kg/min, spec 0.5–1.5), end-point power (4.8 kW, spec 3.5–5.5) — all within validated ranges.
P-01/P-02 PASS: Drying inlet temp 62–63°C (spec 55–70°C), product temp 25–52°C rising within spec 40–55°C at steady state. All readings within range.
P-04 PASS: Process step sequence dispensing → granulation → drying → milling → blending → compression → packaging executed in correct order per MBR.
P-05 PASS: All 5 inter-step hold times within validated limits. Worst: blending→compression at 14h 40min vs 24h limit.
P-07 PASS: Room conditions — RM-101 (21.1–22.8°C, 41–52% RH), RM-201 (21.3–23.1°C, 43–55% RH), RM-401 (20.8–22.6°C, 38–50% RH) — all within spec 18–25°C / ≤60% RH throughout.

logs.message: IPC review complete — 1 MAJOR finding: 3 consecutive compression intervals missing supervisor countersignature.

reasoning_steps: I-01 PASS: LOD tested at 3 locations at drying end — 1.8%, 1.7%, 1.9% — all within spec ≤3.0%.
I-01 PASS: Blend uniformity tested at 3 locations — mean 101.1% LC, RSD 0.3% — within spec ≤5.0% RSD.
I-02 PASS: All 20 compression IPC rows show hardness 6.8–7.1 kP (spec 5–9 kP), weight 60.0–60.3 mg (spec 55–65 mg), friability 0.10–0.12% where tested (spec ≤1.0%). All results within spec.

I-06 FAIL: Compression IPC rows 5, 6, 7 (08:30, 08:45, 09:00) have blank Supervisor Initials (Column J). MBR Section 8.1 and Signatory Matrix require supervisor countersignature at EVERY IPC interval. Three consecutive unsigned intervals is a Major GMP data integrity finding (72-point checklist item 47 flagged N by QA reviewer DK).

I-06 NOTE: Rows 1–4 signed by SM (day supervisor), rows 8–20 signed by KA (night supervisor). The gap at rows 5–7 represents the shift handover window 08:30–09:00 with no supervisor coverage documented.

logs.message: Environmental monitoring confirmed — all rooms within spec throughout the manufacturing campaign.

reasoning_steps: V-01 PASS: RM-101 temp 21.1–22.8°C, RM-201 21.0–23.1°C, RM-401 20.8–22.6°C — all within spec 18–25°C across both manufacturing days.
V-03 PASS: Differential pressure — RM-101 +11 to +17 Pa, RM-201 +11 to +18 Pa (spec +10 to +20 Pa), RM-401 +8 to +13 Pa (spec +5 to +15 Pa) — all within spec.
V-04 PASS: No environmental excursions recorded for any room on either manufacturing day.
V-05 PASS: Monitoring frequency consistent with validated schedule. QA review by D. Krishnaswamy confirmed 22-Mar-2024.

logs.message: Yield and label reconciliation passed — all stage yields in spec, labels 100% accounted for.

reasoning_steps: Y-01/Y-02 PASS: Drying yield 99.8% (spec ≥97.0%), milling yield 99.9% (spec ≥99.0%), blending yield 99.9% (spec ≥99.5%), compression yield 99.5% (spec 97.5–102.5%).

Y-03 PASS: Packaging adjusted yield 98.6% within spec 98.5–101.5% after documented destruction of 68 blisters due to sealing excursion. Deviation PKG-DEV-LIS-2024-031 raised, investigated, and closed.
Y-07 PASS: API accountability 99.8% (spec 98.0–102.0%). Assay 101.4% LC confirms API content within specification.
Y-08 PASS: Label reconciliation — 51,200 issued, 49,755 applied, 1,440 destroyed (documented), 5 returned to store. Discrepancy = 0. QAIP witness P. Venkataraman at 18:05.

logs.message: All 7 QC tests passed — assay 101.4%, dissolution 94.2%, no OOS results raised.

reasoning_steps: QC-01 PASS: Identification (IR) — conforms to reference spectrum.
QC-02 PASS: Assay (HPLC) 101.4% LC — within spec 90.0–110.0%. Content uniformity RSD 1.2% within spec ≤6.0%.
QC-03 PASS: Dissolution (T=30min) 94.2% mean (n=6), all 6 vessels ≥80% — within spec.
QC-04 PASS: Related substances max 0.08% per impurity — within spec ≤0.1% each, ≤0.3% total. Water content 2.1% within spec ≤5.0%. Microbial limits passed.
QC-05 PASS: HPLC LIMS timestamps vs instrument printout — all 13 runs within ±1 second, well within ±2 min tolerance. No data integrity flags. QC supervisor N. Subramaniam reviewed 21–22-Mar-2024.

logs.message: Documentation review complete — 1 MAJOR finding on compression IPC signatures, all other docs complete.

reasoning_steps: D-01 PASS: 72-point batch release checklist completed by QA reviewer D. Krishnaswamy on 22-Mar-2024. 71/72 items pass, 1 item N (item 47 — compression IPC supervisor signatures).
D-02 PASS: All corrections use strikethrough with reason and initials. No white-out detected. All entries attributed to traceable individuals.
D-03 PASS: Step-level operator and supervisor signatures present for granulation, drying, milling, blending, and packaging. QA page sign-offs documented throughout.

D-04 FAIL: Compression IPC Column J (supervisor initials) blank at rows 5, 6, 7. Three consecutive intervals 08:30–09:00 unsigned. MBR and Signatory Matrix require supervisor presence at every IPC check — self-verification is explicitly prohibited.
D-05 PASS: One deviation raised (PKG-DEV-LIS-2024-031) — fully documented, CAPA assigned (CAP-2024-031), closed with QA sign-off by D. Krishnaswamy 21-Mar-2024.

logs.message: Disposition: HOLD — critical BOM formulation discrepancy and product identity mismatch require QA investigation.

reasoning_steps: CRITICAL: EBR records a fundamentally different formulation — Croscarmellose Sodium instead of Crospovidone XL, HPMC E5 instead of Pregelatinised Starch, Mannitol 162.5 kg vs MBR 90 kg, MCC 44.2 kg vs MBR 120 kg. This is not a tolerance deviation — the excipients and quantities do not correspond to MPR-LIS-10-v08.
CRITICAL: EBR product description 'Film-Coated Tablets' contradicts MBR dosage form 'Uncoated tablet, oral'. No coating step is present in the EBR. This is a product identity discrepancy.
MAJOR: 3 consecutive compression IPC supervisor countersignatures missing (rows 5–7, 08:30–09:00). GMP documentation integrity requirement unmet per MBR Section 8.1.

PASS areas: all CPPs within spec, all QC results pass, all hold times within limits, all equipment calibrated, EM all clear, label reconciliation complete, one minor packaging deviation properly closed.

Recommended disposition: HOLD. QA must investigate whether physical manufacture followed MBR or EBR formulation, resolve the film-coated vs uncoated discrepancy, and obtain retrospective supervisor countersignature or raise formal deviation for IPC gap before batch disposition can proceed.

logs.message: Header and administrative review complete — all 12 items compliant.

Batch number LOS-2024-0512 consistent throughout all sections
Batch size 75,000 tablets matches MBR specification exactly
Expiry date May-2027 correctly reflects 36-month shelf life from 14-May-2024
All 72 checklist items reviewed by Diana Walsh on 22-May-2024 with signatures present

logs.message: Dispensing review complete — all 10 materials within tolerance, dual verification confirmed.

reasoning_steps: All 10 raw materials (RM-01 through RM-10) dispensed within ±0.5% tolerance
All AR numbers verified; all expiry dates current at time of use (latest expiry Nov-2025 for Losartan K)
Balance EQ-BAL-001 calibration current (CAL-001-2024, exp 12-Jan-2025)
Double verification present for all dispensed quantities (TK dispensed, VS verified)
Total actual dispensed 15.455 kg vs 15.450 kg target — 0.03% variance, within tolerance

logs.message: All manufacturing CPPs within specification across granulation, drying, milling, blending, compression, coating.

reasoning_steps: Granulation: impeller 205/248 rpm, chopper 1520/1980 rpm, endpoint amperage 21.3 A (spec 18–24 A) — all within range
Drying: inlet 55–56°C (spec 55±5°C), LOD 2.0–2.2% all locations (spec 1.5–3.0%) — pass
Milling: 1.0 mm screen, 1180 rpm (spec 1200±100 rpm), PSD all fractions within specification
Blending: 15 rpm pre-blend 10 min, lubrication 3.0 min (critical limit ≤5 min) — compliant
Compression: main force 14.4–14.6 kN (spec 10–20 kN), all IPC checks passed; coating weight gain 3.4% (spec 2.5–3.5%)

logs.message: All 8 yield stages within target limits — overall yield 98.5%, mass balance acceptable.

reasoning_steps: Wet granulation yield 98.7% (target ≥98.0%) — pass
Drying yield 98.9% (target ≥97.0%), milling yield 99.6% (target ≥98.0%) — both pass
Blending yield 99.1% (target ≥98.5%), compression yield 99.1% (target ≥97.0%) — pass
Coating yield 99.9% (target ≥99.0%), packaging yield 99.6% (target ≥98.0%) — pass
Overall: 73,890 tablets packed (98.5%); unaccounted 0.06% — within 0.5% limit; mass balance acceptable

logs.message: Environmental monitoring compliant across all 8 rooms — particulate, microbial, and water all within limits.

reasoning_steps: All 14 room-condition pairs (at-rest and in-operation) within ISO 8 particulate limits (≤3,520,000/m³ at 0.5µm)
Microbial counts: highest active air result 15 CFU/m³ in MFG-203 (limit ≤200 CFU/m³) — well within limit
Purified water: conductivity 0.8–0.9 µS/cm (≤1.3), TOC 185–210 ppb (≤500 ppb), microbial <10 CFU/mL — all pass
Equipment cleaning pre-use: all swab results 0.6–1.5 ppm (limit ≤10 ppm) — pass
All hold times between manufacturing steps within specified limits (longest: coating→packaging 22.5 hr, limit 60 days)

logs.message: QC testing review identified 1 OOS result: EXP-3179 at 0.24% exceeds NMT 0.20% limit.

reasoning_steps: Assay 100.2% (spec 95–105%), CU AV=5.8 (spec ≤15.0), dissolution mean 91.5% (spec Q≥80%) — all pass
Water content 2.8% (spec ≤5.0%), TAMC 22 CFU/g (spec ≤1000), microbial limits all pass
CRITICAL: EXP-3179 related substance result = 0.24% (duplicate confirmed: 0.24% and 0.24%) — MBR/USP limit NMT 0.20%
All other impurities within limit: hydroxymethyl analog 0.08% (≤0.15%), unknown RRT 0.72 at 0.04% (≤0.10%)
Total impurities 0.38% (spec ≤0.50%) pass — but individual EXP-3179 exceedance is an OOS requiring investigation

logs.message: CRITICAL: OOS investigation not initiated for EXP-3179 failure — batch was released without investigation.

reasoning_steps: EBR Section 13.2 OOS tracking table is completely blank — no OOS number assigned
EBR QA 72-point checklist Item 56 incorrectly marked 'Y' (compliant) for EXP-3179 ≤0.20% despite 0.24% result
SOP-QA-005 requires Phase I investigation initiation immediately upon OOS identification
21 CFR 211.192 requires all discrepancies be fully investigated before batch disposition
Batch was signed for release on 23-May-2024 without any OOS investigation being opened — direct GMP violation

logs.message: Documentation review complete — no deviations logged, all signatures and training records present.

reasoning_steps: All 10 personnel in signature log with Employee IDs, roles, and signatures — complete
All training records current: GMP training (TRN-GMP-2024) and product-specific training verified for all roles
Deviation log shows 'None recorded' — consistent with EBR narrative but inconsistent with OOS finding
No correction fluid used; all appendices (A–P) attached except K (OOS Report) and L (Deviation Reports) — marked N/A
Label reconciliation: 2,600 issued, 2,471 used, 5 damaged, 124 returned — 100% accounted for

logs.message: Findings Summary — 1 Critical, 0 Major, 0 Minor, 0 Observations

data.Finding 2 \u2014 Detail: Batch released without OOS investigation being opened

logs.message: Review complete — HOLD recommended pending OOS investigation and QA checklist correction.

reasoning_steps: EXP-3179 at 0.24% is a confirmed OOS result (duplicate injections both at 0.24%) — not a lab error
Batch cannot be released without a closed OOS investigation per 21 CFR 211.192
QA 72-point checklist Item 56 must be corrected to 'N' with deviation reference
Phase I investigation must determine root cause: storage conditions, synthesis impurity, analytical method issue
Batch placed on HOLD; release gating on OOS closure with documented root cause and CAPA
